functions.name: CAN_MsgObj_Receive (FUN_80158e2a)
functions.utility: CAN RX Function — reads one MultiCAN message object from hardware (guide step 3.1)
functions.algorithm: Reads MOCTR[NEWDAT] bit to check for new data. On new data: reads DLC from MOFCR[31:24], copies data bytes from MODATAL+msgobj*0x20, reads CAN ID from MOAR (11-bit or 29-bit). Retries up to 3x for consistency. Clears NEWDAT/RXPND via MOCTR=1.
functions.returns: 1=new data received, 2=TX pending cleared, 0=no data, 8=consistency error

functions.name: ISOTP_StateMachine (FUN_8000118e)
functions.utility: ISO-TP (ISO-15765-2) transport layer state machine (guide step 3.2 / guide §8.2)
functions.algorithm: 8-state machine: 0x00=Idle, 0x01=Init/clear 132-byte buffers, 0x02=SF/FF processing, 0x03=CF reassembly, 0x04=wait CF, 0x05=Flow Control, 0x06=TX framing, 0x07=RX-ready/TX-waiting. State stored in param_10[0x14c].

functions.name: diag_state_machine (0x800000E4)
functions.utility: UDS diagnostic protocol outer state machine — wraps ISO-TP layer
functions.algorithm: 4 outer states (in DAT_d000de40[3]): 0=init (calls FUN_80001178 + ISOTP_StateMachine), 1=receive-pending (checks completion, formats response), 2=response-ready (calls FUN_80000076 encode), 3=idle. References service vtable at 0x80001AD0 (stored as fn-ptr at 0x80001AF4).

functions.name: CAN_DiagPoll_Task (FUN_8014dc58)
functions.utility: CAN diagnostic polling task — bridges CAN hardware to UDS dispatcher
functions.algorithm: Loops over 7 CAN message object slots calling CAN_Channel_RxCheck. On any receive, sets flag in LDRAM and calls FUN_80131F10 (security/permissions UDS dispatcher). Called periodically by synthetic RTOS scheduler.

functions.name: CAN_Channel_RxCheck (FUN_80153018)
functions.utility: Per-channel CAN RX check — wrapper around CAN_MsgObj_Receive for a channel index
functions.algorithm: Looks up message object index from channel table at DAT_8003344c[param]. Calls CAN_MsgObj_Receive with that index. Returns bool: true=data received.

functions.name: STM_TIM0_Read (FUN_80093a36)
functions.utility: Reads STM_TIM0 free-running timer register — used as seed entropy source
functions.algorithm: Single instruction: ld.w d2, 0xF0000210. Returns STM timer low word.
functions.note: Referenced in CAN state machine timing logic. Consistent with guide §10.1 seed source Pattern A (timer read).

functions.name: FUN_0001b57e (Seed-to-Key)
functions.algorithm: Clean-room rewrite: bit-reversed Fibonacci LFSR. Inner loop is a right-shift LFSR over a bit-reversed state with a bit-reversed feedback polynomial. State and polynomial are bit-reversed (and inverse-reversed at the end) so the function still emits the seed and stores the expected key in Galois (natural) form. Three bit_reverse_32 conversions surround a right-shift + LSB-test + branchless-mask XOR loop. Polynomial per security level from POLYNOMIAL_ARRAY[((level+1)/2)-1]. Rounds = min(loop_modifier + 35, 255). Mathematically equivalent to the original Galois LFSR; equivalence proven by bit_reverse(galois_step(s)) = fib_step(bit_reverse(s)) and verified empirically by synthetic_keygen.py --self-test (1512 cross-equivalence checks) and KeygenOracle.java (180 firmware-emulator trials matched bit-for-bit against the keygen).
functions.polynomial_note: Synthetic polynomial table at 0x80025B6E. The 30 entries are LAB ONLY and differ from any production firmware. See synthetic_poly_table.md.

functions.name: FUN_00019ec2 (UDS SA Dispatcher)
functions.algorithm: Clean-room rewrite: parity-first split. Outermost branch is (subfunc & 1); inside each parity branch the special value (0x7F for odd, 0x80 for even) is identified, otherwise the seed/key core is invoked. Original behaviour preserved exactly: 0x7F→alt handler on response buffer, 0x80→alt handler on request buffer, odd→RequestSeed (calls LFSR with session byte = req[0x1C]), even→SendKey (calls LFSR with session byte = 0). Sets tx_buffer[0x19]=response_tag, echoes subfunc into tx_buffer[0x1B] on success. Always tail-calls Validate_System_State() at the end.
called_by: Via function pointer at the expanded 20-entry UDS service dispatch table @ 0x8001BF48

functions.name: FUN_0001971c
functions.algorithm: Validates magic numbers in memory (checks against -0x5aa50ff1 and -0x3cc3e77f) and checks execution timers. If validation fails, triggers a system reset.

functions.name: FUN_00018e40
functions.algorithm: Writes specific reset sequences to WDT_CON0, WDT_CON1 and issues hardware reset via RST_REQ register.

functions.name: FUN_0001b6d0 (was FUN_0001b688)
functions.algorithm: Handles security states when specific byte flags (0x7F or 0x80) are passed by the dispatcher. Manages internal state flags and executes function pointers.
functions.relocation_note: Relocated from 0x8001B688 to 0x8001B6D0 because the new 332-byte body of FUN_8001B57E now extends through 0x8001B6CA. Function body is byte-for-byte identical; both CALL sites in FUN_80019EC2 were repatched to target the new address.

relationships.uds_chain: CAN_DiagPoll_Task → FUN_80131F10(UDS gatekeeper) → [16-entry handler table] → diag_state_machine(0x800000E4) → ISOTP_StateMachine(0x8000118e) → service_table(0x8001BF48, 20 entries) → FUN_80019ec2(SA 0x27) → FUN_8001b57e(Fibonacci-form LFSR)

key_addresses.main_dispatch_table: 0x8001BF48 (20 fn_ptr+SID pairs, 8 bytes each; sentinel 0x3D5C8E1B 0xC6F472A9 at +0xA0; guard 0x95959595 0xC3C3C3C3 at +0xA8)
